reasoning: The September 2025 FOMC meeting resulted in a rate cut (25 basis points), fulfilling the first 'Cut'. For the October 2025 meeting, CME FedWatch and futures markets price in a 96-98% probability of another rate cut, strongly supporting the second 'Cut'. For the December 2025 meeting, market probabilities indicate a roughly 70-78.8% chance of a further cut, leaving about a 22-30% probability of a policy pause. This is supported by the Fed's "dot plot" which shows a nearly even split among officials (10 favoring two more cuts this year, 9 favoring only one or zero more cuts). Given this backdrop—combined with uncertainty over potential government shutdown impacts on data and lingering "sticky inflation" concerns flagged by several major banks—the probability of a December pause conditional on an October cut is around 24-25%. Therefore, the likelihood of exactly a Cut-Cut-Pause sequence rests securely around 24%.
